name: The Witness
description:
The Witness

*{Lore The Witness remembers what others bury. Every shadow cast and oath broken is a line in her unending ledger. She is the keeper of inconvenient truths, the patron of those who seek to expose lies or recover forgotten knowledge. Her followers learn that knowledge comes with a price --- the weight of remembering what others would forget.

The Witness has no will, only a ledger; no mercy, only accuracy. She does not demand confessions, but she ensures that every lie leaves a stain, every secret a trace. Those who serve her become archivists of the uncomfortable, witnesses to the truths that others would kill to keep hidden. To walk her path is to accept that some truths are weapons, and that the truth-teller is never loved.

Among the peoples of Fate's Edge, the Witness is known by many names. In Ecktoria, she is the Ink-Eye, the Censor's clerk who copies every redacted line onto a private scroll. In Vhasia, she is the Fractured Mirror, prayed to by spies who seek to reveal a rival's true loyalty. In the Mistlands, she is the Fog-Scribe, whose name is whispered when a bell-line fails and the dead must be identified by their secrets. And in the shadows of every great city, there are those who follow the Apocalyptic Witness --- a doomsaying cult that believes the world will end when the last hidden truth is finally spoken aloud, and who work to hasten that revelation by exposing every lie, no matter the cost.

quote "I will show you what you would rather forget. But first, you must forget what you think you know." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Truth Revelation: exposing lies, uncovering secrets, forcing confessions Memory Keeping: preserving knowledge, recalling forgotten facts Inconvenient Knowledge: learning what others wish to hide Prophetic Warning: seeing the consequences of hidden truths Burden of Witness: the psychological weight of knowing too much itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to the Witness does not bind a familiar of flesh and comfort. Their Thiasos is a small creature that embodies memory and revelation: a raven that collects shiny objects and recites forgotten names, a silverfish that eats lies from parchment and leaves truth behind, a small brass beetle whose shell opens to reveal a perfect mirror, a white mouse that nests in archive drawers and remembers every page it has slept upon, or a cricket that chirps in the key of the last secret spoken nearby. This creature requires no food, but it must witness something significant (a lie told, a secret revealed, a confession made) each week. If neglected, it becomes Compromised (it grows silent and still, and its bearer loses +1 die on all Investigation rolls until it is shown a hidden truth).

The Codex of the Witness is never a book that can be closed. It may be a set of loose-leaf pages that reorganize themselves each dawn, a scroll that writes itself when no one is looking, a chalkboard that cannot be erased, or a stack of wax tablets that melt and reset each season. Because the Witness values accuracy over permanence, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week correcting a single error in its records (a misspelled name, a misdated event) or the Rites stored there become garbled (treat as Compromised until restored).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of the Witness may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or writing implement) and whisper a word of truth. For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Insight or Investigation rolls, and the first time each scene the bearer would be deceived by an illusion or a lie, they may roll twice and take the better result. After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to the Witness.
summary: The Witness *{Lore The Witness remembers what others bury. Every shadow cast and oath broken is a line in her unending ledger. She is the keeper of inconvenient truths, the patron of those who seek to expose lies or recover forgotten knowledge.

rites.name: Rite of the Lingering Trace
rites.description: (Basic, 4 XP) Instant; Near; Yes (Investigation/Notice only). Tags: , Materials: A trace of the thing to be remembered (hair, dust, a spoken name). Effect: You follow a hidden thread. Gain +1 die to investigate or notice something directly related to the trace within the current scene. Additionally, you may ask the GM one yes/no question about whether the trace is connected to a specific person, event, or object. Push It: Gain +2 dice instead, but mark 1 segment on a Memory Strain Timer [4]. If the timer fills, you gain Fatigue 1 and suffer --1 die on Investigation/Notice rolls until the end of the next scene (the Witness taxes your mind). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: You follow a hidden thread. Gain +1 die to investigate or notice something directly related to the trace within the current scene. Additionally, you may ask the GM one yes/no question about whether the trace is connected to a specific person, event, or object. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Uncomfortable Question
rites.description: (Basic, 5 XP) Scene; Near; No. Tags: , , Materials: A circle drawn with chalk or string while focusing on a specific person. Effect: You force a moment of raw honesty. Ask one question of a target within the zone. They may either answer truthfully (the GM must give a true answer, though it may be cryptic) or mark 1 Fatigue and gain a Guilty Tell Condition (--1 die to all social rolls until they confess the truth to someone). Push It: The target must also reveal a surface emotion or a forgotten detail (GM's choice). Mark 1 SB (Hearts) as the Witness records the strain. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: You force a moment of raw honesty. Ask one question of a target within the zone. They may either answer truthfully (the GM must give a true answer, though it may be cryptic) or mark 1 Fatigue and gain a Guilty Tell Condition (--1 die to all social rolls until they confess the truth to someone). \\

rites.name: Rite of the Echoing Truth
rites.description: (Advanced, 8 XP) Instant; Near; No. Tags: , , Materials: A reflective surface (mirror, still water, polished metal) used to focus on the target. Effect: The target must make a Resolve test (DV 3) or suffer --1 die to rolls involving memory, deception, or resisting interrogation for the scene. If they fail, you may ask one specific, factual question about something they know, and they must answer truthfully or suffer 1 SB (Hearts) as the memory is forcibly drawn forth. Push It: If the target fails their Resolve test, you may ask a second question, but the mental intrusion causes them Harm 1 (Stress). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: The target must make a Resolve test (DV 3) or suffer --1 die to rolls involving memory, deception, or resisting interrogation for the scene. If they fail, you may ask one specific, factual question about something they know, and they must answer truthfully or suffer 1 SB (Hearts) as the memory is forcibly drawn forth. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Immutable Record
rites.description: (Advanced, 7 XP) Scene; Near; No. Tags: , , Materials: A document signed by all parties within the zone, or a spoken pact witnessed by the caster. Effect: Bind the agreement. Any party who knowingly breaches it suffers 1 SB (Hearts) immediately and gains a persistent Oathbreaker's Mark Condition (--1 die on social rolls involving honor, trust, or oaths until amends are made or a significant act redeems them). Push It: The bond becomes magically enforced for one specific, crucial clause: violation automatically inflicts Harm 1 (Stress) on the breaker in addition to the SB and Mark. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Campaign (once).
rites.effect: Bind the agreement. Any party who knowingly breaches it suffers 1 SB (Hearts) immediately and gains a persistent Oathbreaker's Mark Condition (--1 die on social rolls involving honor, trust, or oaths until amends are made or a significant act redeems them). \\

rites.name: Rite of the Shared Burden
rites.description: (Advanced, 9 XP) Instant; Touch; No. Tags: , , Materials: A written secret (yours or another's) and a candle. Effect: You transfer the weight of a truth. Choose one: you may remove a Guilty Tell, Haunted, or similar psychological Condition from yourself and give it to a willing or unwilling target (they resist with Resolve DV 4). Alternatively, you may share a secret with an ally; both of you clear 1 Fatigue as the burden is halved. Push It: Transfer a Condition without the target's consent (they automatically resist, but on failure they gain it). Mark 1 SB (Spades) as the secret stains them. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Instant.
rites.effect: You transfer the weight of a truth. Choose one: you may remove a Guilty Tell, Haunted, or similar psychological Condition from yourself and give it to a willing or unwilling target (they resist with Resolve DV 4). Alternatively, you may share a secret with an ally; both of you clear 1 Fatigue as the burden is halved. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Unveiled Heart
rites.description: (Master, 12 XP) Scene; Near; No. Tags: , , , Materials: A private setting where the target feels safe or is speaking freely. Effect: The target suffers --2 dice to all attempts to conceal true emotions, intentions, or lies for the scene. Any successful social roll (Sway, Command, Deceit) made by the target generates 1 SB (Hearts) as the effort to maintain falsehoods under the Witness's gaze creates internal discord. Push It: You may designate one specific, complex question about the target's motivations, fears, or hidden loyalties. If you successfully use Sway or Insight against them this scene, you automatically learn the answer to that question. The intense scrutiny marks 1 SB (Spades) for you as the Witness's attention lingers. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: The target suffers --2 dice to all attempts to conceal true emotions, intentions, or lies for the scene. Any successful social roll (Sway, Command, Deceit) made by the target generates 1 SB (Hearts) as the effort to maintain falsehoods under the Witness's gaze creates internal discord. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Final Reckoning
rites.description: (Master, 13 XP) Scene; Zone; No. Tags: , , , Materials: A formally called gathering (court, council, family meeting) within the consecrated zone. Effect: All present must speak their greatest debt, wrongdoing, or hidden truth related to the gathering's purpose. Those who lie or withhold suffer Harm 2 (Stress/Reputation). Truth-tellers gain +2 dice to social actions for the remainder of the scene within the zone. Push It: The truth becomes inescapable --- even indirect lies or evasions related to the core topic suffer the Harm 2 penalty. The absolute nature of the revelation creates 2 SB (Diamonds) as the disruption to fates and secrets resonates. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: All present must speak their greatest debt, wrongdoing, or hidden truth related to the gathering's purpose. Those who lie or withhold suffer Harm 2 (Stress/Reputation). Truth-tellers gain +2 dice to social actions for the remainder of the scene within the zone. \\
